tasks.title: Stabilize Mission Control local runtime and restart path
tasks.notes: Syntax blocker is fixed, repeat restart path is hardened, and localhost:3000 is serving again. Next polish pass is tighter PID hygiene and cleaner shutdown behavior.

tasks.title: Rebuild Task Board into a truthful current operating queue
tasks.notes: Remove stale demo-era sprint residue and align the board to current Mission Control stabilization, runtime trust, and visibility work.

tasks.title: Align Now, Task Board, and Office to the same operational story
tasks.notes: The top surfaces should reflect the same current priorities, live runtime state, blocker language, and source-of-truth boundaries.

tasks.title: Clean remaining stale hardcoded status theater from Mission Control surfaces
tasks.notes: Audit Now, Office, Team, Projects, Docs, and Memory surfaces for legacy snapshot content that still looks live but is not trustworthy.

tasks.title: Define trusted source boundaries per Mission Control screen
tasks.notes: Each major surface should clearly map to live runtime data, file-backed data, Supabase-native state, or explicit fallback state.

tasks.title: Harden live runtime visibility for permanent agent lanes
tasks.notes: Office and Now should show real lane freshness, live/stale state, and useful last-output summaries without pretending to know more than the data source supports.

tasks.title: Preserve GHL Pipeline as a strong live surface while tightening weaker screens
tasks.notes: Do not tear down the strongest current surface. Keep the working live pipeline view and improve the weaker visibility layers around it.

tasks.title: Document the clean Mission Control startup path for continuity
tasks.notes: Make the local bring-up path obvious so runtime recovery does not depend on chat history or memory.
